title: Day 13 Vocabulary — Use of Let (100+ Words)

words.simpleMeaning: to permit someone to do something

sentences.daily: My parents allow me to go out on weekends.
sentences.office: The manager allows flexible working hours.
sentences.interview: I allow my team members to take initiative.
sentences.business: We allow clients to pay in installments.
sentences.formal: The policy allows employees to work from home twice a week.
sentences.informal: Will you allow me to sit here?

words.usageNote: 'Allow' is more formal than 'let'. Allow is followed by 'to + verb': allow him to go.

words.simpleMeaning: to officially allow something

sentences.daily: My landlord permits pets in the flat.
sentences.office: The office permits casual dress on Fridays.
sentences.interview: I permit my colleagues to use my resources freely.
sentences.business: The contract permits early termination.
sentences.formal: Smoking is not permitted on the premises.
sentences.informal: Will he permit us to leave early?

words.usageNote: 'Permit' is more formal than 'allow'. Followed by 'to + verb'.

words.simpleMeaning: to officially say that something must not be done

sentences.daily: My mother forbids me from eating junk food.
sentences.office: Company rules forbid sharing passwords.
sentences.interview: I never forbid my team from sharing new ideas.
sentences.business: The agreement forbids sharing client data.
sentences.formal: It is forbidden to enter without ID.
sentences.informal: He forbade us from going near the river.

words.usageNote: Irregular: forbid → forbade → forbidden. More formal/literary than 'not allow'.

words.simpleMeaning: to stop something from happening

sentences.daily: Wear a helmet to prevent head injuries.
sentences.office: Regular backups prevent data loss.
sentences.interview: I take steps to prevent miscommunication in the team.
sentences.business: Quality checks prevent product defects.
sentences.formal: The medicine prevents infection from spreading.
sentences.informal: Don't let him prevent you from trying.

words.usageNote: 'Prevent from + ing': prevent him from leaving.

words.simpleMeaning: to put a limit on something

sentences.daily: The doctor restricted my diet after surgery.
sentences.office: Access to the server room is restricted.
sentences.interview: I don't restrict my team unnecessarily.
sentences.business: The government restricted imports.
sentences.formal: Restricted areas require special clearance.
sentences.informal: Don't let them restrict your growth.

words.usageNote: 'Restrict' implies setting boundaries or limits.

words.simpleMeaning: to make a choice after thinking

sentences.daily: Let me decide what to cook today.
sentences.office: Let the team decide the project timeline.
sentences.interview: I always let facts decide my actions.
sentences.business: Let the data decide our strategy.
sentences.formal: The board will decide on the merger next week.
sentences.informal: Let him decide — it's his call.

words.usageNote: 'Decide on' = to make a final choice. 'Decide to do': I decided to leave.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something clear or easy to understand

sentences.daily: Let me explain how this works.
sentences.office: Let her explain the new process.
sentences.interview: Let me explain my experience in detail.
sentences.business: Let us explain our pricing structure.
sentences.formal: Please allow me to explain the terms.
sentences.informal: Let me explain — it wasn't my fault!

words.usageNote: 'Explain to someone': explain this to me (not 'explain me this' in formal English).

words.simpleMeaning: to deal with or manage something

sentences.daily: Let me handle the shopping today.
sentences.office: Let him handle the client complaint.
sentences.interview: I can handle pressure and tight deadlines.
sentences.business: Let our team handle your logistics.
sentences.formal: The matter was handled professionally.
sentences.informal: Let me handle it — don't worry.

words.usageNote: 'Handle' implies taking control/responsibility. 'Handle pressure' is a common phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: to show or introduce something formally

sentences.daily: Let me present my idea to the family.
sentences.office: Let her present the quarterly report.
sentences.interview: Let me present my key achievements.
sentences.business: Let us present our proposal.
sentences.formal: He was asked to present his findings.
sentences.informal: Let him present his side of the story.

words.usageNote: 'Present' as noun = gift / the current time. As verb = to show formally.

words.simpleMeaning: to state or show that something is true or certain

sentences.daily: Let me confirm the address before we leave.
sentences.office: Let him confirm the booking.
sentences.interview: Let me confirm — you're asking about my management experience?
sentences.business: Let us confirm the order details.
sentences.formal: Please confirm your attendance by Friday.
sentences.informal: Let me just confirm with her first.

words.usageNote: 'Confirm' implies making something definite or certain.

words.simpleMeaning: to finish something

sentences.daily: Let me complete my homework first.
sentences.office: Let them complete the task by 5 PM.
sentences.interview: I always complete projects on time.
sentences.business: Let us complete the formalities.
sentences.formal: Please complete the form and submit it.
sentences.informal: Don't let him leave before he completes the job.

words.usageNote: 'Complete' is more formal than 'finish'. Can be used as adjective: a complete answer.

words.simpleMeaning: to go into a place

sentences.daily: Don't let the cat enter the kitchen.
sentences.office: Please don't let visitors enter without a pass.
sentences.interview: I entered the company three years ago.
sentences.business: Enter your PIN to access the account.
sentences.formal: No one shall enter without authorization.
sentences.informal: Don't let him enter my room.

words.usageNote: 'Enter into' an agreement = formally start a contract. 'Enter' a room = go inside.

words.simpleMeaning: to stop someone while they are speaking or working

sentences.daily: Don't let him interrupt me while I am studying.
sentences.office: Let me finish — please don't interrupt.
sentences.interview: I never interrupt when someone is speaking.
sentences.business: Don't let small issues interrupt the workflow.
sentences.formal: Interruptions are not permitted during the presentation.
sentences.informal: Don't interrupt — let her speak!

words.usageNote: 'Interrupt' is rude in most contexts. 'May I interrupt for a moment?' is polite.

words.simpleMeaning: to continue doing something or to go forward

sentences.daily: Let us proceed with the plan.
sentences.office: Let the team proceed with the project.
sentences.interview: Please let me proceed — I will answer all your questions.
sentences.business: You may proceed once the payment clears.
sentences.formal: The court allowed the case to proceed.
sentences.informal: Let him proceed — we'll see what happens.

words.usageNote: 'Proceed' is formal. In speech, 'go ahead' is more common.

words.simpleMeaning: to officially agree or give permission

sentences.daily: Let the senior approve my leave application.
sentences.office: Let the manager approve the budget first.
sentences.interview: I can approve decisions within my authority.
sentences.business: Let the board approve the merger.
sentences.formal: The committee approved the proposal unanimously.
sentences.informal: Don't let him approve anything without checking.

words.usageNote: 'Approve of' = like/support. 'Approve' = officially permit.

words.simpleMeaning: to get free from a difficult situation

sentences.daily: Don't let the bird escape from the cage.
sentences.office: Don't let errors escape unnoticed.
sentences.interview: I never let problems escape my attention.
sentences.business: Don't let any lead escape the pipeline.
sentences.formal: Security measures prevent unauthorized escape.
sentences.informal: Don't let the cat escape — close the door!

words.usageNote: 'Escape from' = get out of. 'Escape notice' = not be noticed.

words.simpleMeaning: to show or tell something hidden

sentences.daily: Let me reveal a small secret.
sentences.office: Don't let confidential data be revealed.
sentences.interview: Let me reveal what I learned from that challenge.
sentences.business: Don't let competitors reveal your pricing.
sentences.formal: The investigation revealed serious lapses.
sentences.informal: Let him reveal the surprise — it's his idea.

words.usageNote: 'Reveal' implies showing something hidden. More dramatic than 'show'.

words.simpleMeaning: to resolve something or to make a place your home

sentences.daily: Let me settle this argument once and for all.
sentences.office: Let them settle the payment terms.
sentences.interview: Let me settle into the role first.
sentences.business: Let us settle this dispute out of court.
sentences.formal: The matter has been settled amicably.
sentences.informal: Don't let this unsettled issue linger.

words.usageNote: 'Settle down' = calm down or establish a stable life. 'Settle for' = accept less than wanted.

words.simpleMeaning: to pay no attention to something

sentences.daily: Don't let him ignore his health.
sentences.office: Don't let small mistakes be ignored.
sentences.interview: I never ignore feedback from my managers.
sentences.business: Don't let customer complaints be ignored.
sentences.formal: The committee urged parties not to ignore the evidence.
sentences.informal: Let her ignore it — it's not worth it.

words.usageNote: 'Ignore' implies a deliberate choice to not pay attention.

words.simpleMeaning: to focus your attention on something

sentences.daily: Let me concentrate on this chapter.
sentences.office: Let him concentrate — don't disturb him.
sentences.interview: I concentrate on delivering quality results.
sentences.business: Let us concentrate on the core business.
sentences.formal: Employees are asked to concentrate on key deliverables.
sentences.informal: Don't let them distract you — concentrate!

words.usageNote: 'Concentrate on' = focus on. Also used in chemistry (concentrated solution).

words.simpleMeaning: to put forward an idea for consideration

sentences.daily: Let me suggest a better route.
sentences.office: Let her suggest improvements in the process.
sentences.interview: Let me suggest how I can add value here.
sentences.business: Let us suggest a revised pricing model.
sentences.formal: May I suggest an alternative approach?
sentences.informal: Let him suggest whatever he wants.

words.usageNote: 'Suggest + ing' or 'suggest + that clause'. NOT 'suggest to do'.

words.simpleMeaning: to watch carefully

sentences.daily: Let me observe how you do it first.
sentences.office: Let her observe the process before starting.
sentences.interview: I observe and learn from experienced colleagues.
sentences.business: Let us observe market behavior this quarter.
sentences.formal: The team was asked to observe safety protocols.
sentences.informal: Let him observe — he's new here.

words.usageNote: 'Observe' is more formal than 'watch'. Also means to follow rules: observe rules.

words.simpleMeaning: to confess or allow entry

sentences.daily: Let me admit that I made a mistake.
sentences.office: Let them admit their error and move on.
sentences.interview: I readily admit my weaknesses and work on them.
sentences.business: Don't let a wrong admission hurt the company.
sentences.formal: He was admitted to the hospital for treatment.
sentences.informal: Just let him admit it — everyone already knows.

words.usageNote: 'Admit to' = confess. 'Be admitted' = allowed to enter (hospital/college).

words.simpleMeaning: to find the answer to a problem

sentences.daily: Let me solve this math problem first.
sentences.office: Let the experts solve the technical issue.
sentences.interview: Let me explain how I solve complex problems.
sentences.business: Let data solve your business challenges.
sentences.formal: The committee was asked to solve the dispute.
sentences.informal: Let him solve it — he's good at these things.

words.usageNote: 'Solve' is for problems. 'Resolve' is for conflicts. Both are correct in many contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: to guide or be in charge of a group

sentences.daily: Let me lead the family trip planning.
sentences.office: Let her lead the new project.
sentences.interview: Let me lead you through my key achievements.
sentences.business: Let the expert lead the negotiation.
sentences.formal: The CEO asked her to lead the restructuring.
sentences.informal: Let him lead — he knows the way.

words.usageNote: Irregular: lead → led → led. 'Lead' (noun) = the front position. 'Lead' (metal) = /led/.

words.simpleMeaning: to arrange things in an orderly way

sentences.daily: Let me organize my desk before starting.
sentences.office: Let him organize the farewell party.
sentences.interview: I let my team organize their own schedules.
sentences.business: Let us organize a client appreciation event.
sentences.formal: The event was organized by the HR department.
sentences.informal: Don't let things get disorganized.

words.usageNote: British spelling: 'organise'. Both are correct.

words.simpleMeaning: to be in charge of or to handle successfully

sentences.daily: Let me manage the household budget.
sentences.office: Let him manage the junior team.
sentences.interview: I can manage multiple projects simultaneously.
sentences.business: Let an expert manage your finances.
sentences.formal: The operations are managed by a senior director.
sentences.informal: Don't worry — let me manage it.

words.usageNote: 'Manage to do' = succeed in doing despite difficulty: I managed to finish on time.

words.simpleMeaning: to free from constraint or to make available

sentences.daily: Don't let him release the news before the right time.
sentences.office: Let the team release the update after testing.
sentences.interview: I helped release a product that served 10,000 users.
sentences.business: Let us release the product next quarter.
sentences.formal: The prisoner was released on bail.
sentences.informal: Let him release his frustration — he needs it.

words.usageNote: 'Release' used for software updates, news, emotions, prisoners.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep doing something without stopping

sentences.daily: Let me continue my story.
sentences.office: Let the work continue despite the holiday.
sentences.interview: Let me continue — I have more to share.
sentences.business: Don't let the issue continue unresolved.
sentences.formal: The project will continue as scheduled.
sentences.informal: Just let him continue — we'll ask questions later.

words.usageNote: 'Continue to do' or 'continue doing' — both correct.

words.simpleMeaning: the right or ability to use or enter something

sentences.daily: Don't let unauthorized people access your phone.
sentences.office: Let the admin access the shared drive.
sentences.interview: I have access to several data analytics tools.
sentences.business: Let clients access the portal 24/7.
sentences.formal: Access to the building is restricted after 8 PM.
sentences.informal: Don't let her access your private account.

words.usageNote: As noun: 'access to'. As verb: 'access the file'.

words.simpleMeaning: to use more of something than is necessary

sentences.daily: Don't let him waste food.
sentences.office: Don't let meetings waste productive time.
sentences.interview: I don't let pressure waste my focus.
sentences.business: Don't let budget overruns waste resources.
sentences.formal: Unnecessary delays waste valuable time.
sentences.informal: Don't let him waste your time.

words.usageNote: 'Waste of time/money' is a common phrase. 'Waste away' = become weak gradually.

words.simpleMeaning: to take someone's attention away from what they are doing

sentences.daily: Don't let the noise distract you from studying.
sentences.office: Don't let them distract the team.
sentences.interview: I don't let interruptions distract me.
sentences.business: Don't let small problems distract from the big picture.
sentences.formal: Irrelevant issues should not distract the committee.
sentences.informal: Don't let him distract you — keep working.

words.usageNote: 'Distracted by' = attention is taken by something.

words.simpleMeaning: to include someone in an activity

sentences.daily: Let me involve my brother in this decision.
sentences.office: Let us involve all departments in the planning.
sentences.interview: I always involve stakeholders early.
sentences.business: Let clients be involved in product development.
sentences.formal: All relevant parties must be involved.
sentences.informal: Don't let him be left out — involve him.

words.usageNote: 'Involve in' = make someone part of. 'Be involved in' = participate in.

words.simpleMeaning: to give someone information about something

sentences.daily: Let me inform my parents about this.
sentences.office: Let HR inform the team about the new policy.
sentences.interview: I kept my team informed at all times.
sentences.business: Let us inform our clients of the change.
sentences.formal: All staff must be informed of the new regulation.
sentences.informal: Don't let him be uninformed — tell him.

words.usageNote: 'Inform of/about' = tell someone. 'Informed decision' = based on knowledge.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep someone or something safe from harm

sentences.daily: Don't let anyone protect a wrongdoer.
sentences.office: Let the security team protect office assets.
sentences.interview: I protect the company's data with strong passwords.
sentences.business: Let us protect our brand reputation.
sentences.formal: The law is designed to protect consumers.
sentences.informal: Don't let him protect the bully.

words.usageNote: 'Protect from/against': protect children from harm.

words.simpleMeaning: to rely on someone or something

sentences.daily: Don't let your happiness depend on others.
sentences.office: Let the timeline depend on the client's requirements.
sentences.interview: My success depends on my preparation.
sentences.business: Let growth depend on customer satisfaction.
sentences.formal: Results depend on the quality of inputs.
sentences.informal: Don't let your mood depend on his mood.

words.usageNote: 'Depend on' = rely on. 'It depends' = it varies (without 'on' in casual speech).

words.simpleMeaning: to do something very quickly

sentences.daily: Don't let him rush through his meals.
sentences.office: Don't let the team rush the quality checks.
sentences.interview: I never rush my answers in an interview.
sentences.business: Don't let urgency rush a bad decision.
sentences.formal: Rushed decisions often lead to poor outcomes.
sentences.informal: Don't rush me — let me think.

words.usageNote: 'Rush hour' = peak traffic time. 'In a rush' = in a hurry.

words.simpleMeaning: to fail to meet someone's expectations

sentences.daily: Don't let him disappoint his parents.
sentences.office: Don't let a small mistake disappoint the client.
sentences.interview: I never let my team down.
sentences.business: Don't let poor service disappoint customers.
sentences.formal: The company strives never to disappoint its stakeholders.
sentences.informal: Don't let her disappoint you again.

words.usageNote: 'Disappoint' someone = let them down. 'Be disappointed in/with' someone.

words.simpleMeaning: to make someone feel enthusiastic to do something

sentences.daily: Let positive stories motivate you.
sentences.office: Let the manager motivate the team.
sentences.interview: I am motivated by challenges.
sentences.business: Let customer feedback motivate product improvements.
sentences.formal: Leadership must motivate employees to excel.
sentences.informal: Let him motivate himself — don't force it.

words.usageNote: 'Motivated by' = driven by a reason. 'Motivate to do' = encourage to act.

words.simpleMeaning: to concentrate on one thing

sentences.daily: Let me focus on one task at a time.
sentences.office: Let the team focus on the final delivery.
sentences.interview: Let me focus on my biggest achievement.
sentences.business: Let us focus on profitability this quarter.
sentences.formal: The workshop helps participants focus on priorities.
sentences.informal: Don't let distractions break your focus.

words.usageNote: 'Focus on' = pay attention to. 'In focus / out of focus' (photography).

words.simpleMeaning: to question or test something; a difficult task

sentences.daily: Let life's challenges make you stronger.
sentences.office: Let him challenge the status quo.
sentences.interview: Don't let a challenge become an excuse.
sentences.business: Let us challenge our competitors with better quality.
sentences.formal: The team was challenged to exceed targets.
sentences.informal: Let me challenge you — can you do 50 questions today?

words.usageNote: 'Challenge' as noun = a difficult task. As verb = to question or dare.

words.simpleMeaning: to discuss to reach an agreement

sentences.daily: Let me negotiate a better price.
sentences.office: Let the senior manager negotiate the contract.
sentences.interview: I have negotiated vendor contracts worth ₹50 lakh.
sentences.business: Let us negotiate the terms before signing.
sentences.formal: Both parties negotiated in good faith.
sentences.informal: Don't let them negotiate without you.

words.usageNote: 'Negotiate with' someone, 'negotiate for' something.

words.simpleMeaning: to give part of your work to someone else

sentences.daily: Let me delegate some tasks to save time.
sentences.office: Let him delegate work to the junior team.
sentences.interview: I believe in delegating to build team capability.
sentences.business: Let the manager delegate routine work.
sentences.formal: Effective leaders delegate responsibilities wisely.
sentences.informal: Let her delegate — she can't do everything alone.

words.usageNote: 'Delegate to' = assign to. A 'delegate' (noun) = a representative at a conference.

words.simpleMeaning: to watch and check something regularly

sentences.daily: Let me monitor my child's screen time.
sentences.office: Let the team lead monitor daily progress.
sentences.interview: I monitor project metrics regularly.
sentences.business: Let us monitor sales on a weekly basis.
sentences.formal: All employees' performance is monitored quarterly.
sentences.informal: Don't let issues go unmonitored.

words.usageNote: 'Monitor' implies ongoing, regular checking.

words.simpleMeaning: to assess the quality or value of something

sentences.daily: Let me evaluate all options before deciding.
sentences.office: Let the panel evaluate the candidates.
sentences.interview: Let me evaluate what this role needs.
sentences.business: Let data evaluate our marketing ROI.
sentences.formal: Performance is evaluated annually.
sentences.informal: Let him evaluate it — he has the expertise.

words.usageNote: 'Evaluate' is more formal than 'assess'. Both followed by direct object.

words.simpleMeaning: to change an event to a later time

sentences.daily: Don't let him postpone his studies.
sentences.office: Let us postpone the meeting to tomorrow.
sentences.interview: I never postpone important decisions.
sentences.business: Don't let the launch be postponed without reason.
sentences.formal: The hearing was postponed due to unavailability.
sentences.informal: Don't let them postpone it again.

words.usageNote: 'Postpone to' a future date. 'Postpone until' = delay until.

words.simpleMeaning: to formally leave a job

sentences.daily: Don't let frustration push him to resign.
sentences.office: Let him resign if he wants — that's his right.
sentences.interview: I resigned to pursue better opportunities.
sentences.business: Don't let a key employee resign without knowing why.
sentences.formal: She resigned from her post with one month's notice.
sentences.informal: Don't let small issues make you resign.

words.usageNote: 'Resign from' a position. 'Be resigned to' = accept something unwillingly.

words.simpleMeaning: to give someone confidence to do something

sentences.daily: Let me encourage him to try again.
sentences.office: Let the manager encourage creative thinking.
sentences.interview: I believe in encouraging my teammates.
sentences.business: Let positive reviews encourage more customers.
sentences.formal: Teachers are encouraged to let students explore.
sentences.informal: Don't let him give up — encourage him!

words.usageNote: 'Encourage to do': encourage him to speak up.

words.simpleMeaning: to go somewhere with someone

sentences.daily: Let me accompany you to the hospital.
sentences.office: Let her accompany the senior officer.
sentences.interview: I accompanied my manager to three client meetings.
sentences.business: Let our representative accompany you on the site visit.
sentences.formal: Guests were accompanied to their seats.
sentences.informal: Let him accompany us — he knows the area.

words.usageNote: 'Accompany' is more formal than 'go with'.

words.simpleMeaning: to show feelings or thoughts in words

sentences.daily: Let me express my gratitude.
sentences.office: Let him express his opinion in the meeting.
sentences.interview: Let me express why I am perfect for this role.
sentences.business: Let your brand express its values clearly.
sentences.formal: Employees are encouraged to express concerns freely.
sentences.informal: Don't let your fear stop you from expressing yourself.

words.usageNote: 'Express yourself' = communicate who you are. 'Express delivery' = fast shipping.

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully reach a goal

sentences.daily: Let hard work help you achieve your dreams.
sentences.office: Let the team achieve their targets.
sentences.interview: Let me tell you what I have achieved.
sentences.business: Let results achieve themselves through good process.
sentences.formal: The project achieved all its milestones.
sentences.informal: Don't let fear stop you from achieving.

words.usageNote: 'Achieve' implies effort. 'Achieve a goal/target/result'.

words.simpleMeaning: to pause because of uncertainty

sentences.daily: Don't let him hesitate — the time is now.
sentences.office: Don't let uncertainty make you hesitate.
sentences.interview: Please don't hesitate to ask me anything.
sentences.business: Don't let clients hesitate — give them a clear CTA.
sentences.formal: She hesitated before signing the document.
sentences.informal: Don't hesitate — let me know if you need help.

words.usageNote: 'Hesitate to do': I don't hesitate to ask questions.

words.simpleMeaning: to become aware of something

sentences.daily: Let me realize my own potential.
sentences.office: Let him realize his mistake on his own.
sentences.interview: I realized early that communication is key.
sentences.business: Let the market realize the value of your product.
sentences.formal: The team realized the gravity of the situation.
sentences.informal: Don't let it be too late when he realizes.

words.usageNote: British: 'realise'. Both correct. 'Realize + that clause'.

words.simpleMeaning: to say or do something in reply

sentences.daily: Let me respond to his message.
sentences.office: Let her respond to client queries.
sentences.interview: Let me respond to that question with an example.
sentences.business: Don't let customer emails go without a response.
sentences.formal: All complaints must be responded to within 48 hours.
sentences.informal: Let him respond — give him time.

words.usageNote: 'Respond to' something. More formal than 'reply'.

words.simpleMeaning: to put money or time into something for future benefit

sentences.daily: Let me invest in my education first.
sentences.office: Let the company invest in employee training.
sentences.interview: I invest time in learning new skills.
sentences.business: Let us invest in digital marketing.
sentences.formal: Investors are encouraged to invest wisely.
sentences.informal: Don't let him invest in something risky.

words.usageNote: 'Invest in' something. 'Investment' is the noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to not pay attention to something

sentences.daily: Don't let him ignore his health issues.
sentences.office: Don't ignore feedback from your team.
sentences.interview: I never ignore customer pain points.
sentences.business: Don't let quality issues be ignored.
sentences.formal: It would be unwise to ignore these findings.
sentences.informal: Don't let her ignore you — speak up.

words.usageNote: Ignoring is a deliberate action, unlike 'not noticing' which can be unintentional.

words.simpleMeaning: a chance to do something beneficial

sentences.daily: Don't let this opportunity pass.
sentences.office: Let every challenge be an opportunity.
sentences.interview: This role is a great opportunity for me.
sentences.business: Don't let market opportunities go unexplored.
sentences.formal: The management presented a unique opportunity.
sentences.informal: Don't let him miss this opportunity!

words.usageNote: 'Take the opportunity to do' = use the chance. 'Miss an opportunity'.

words.simpleMeaning: the right to do something

sentences.daily: Let me ask for permission first.
sentences.office: Let the department head grant permission.
sentences.interview: I always seek permission before taking major steps.
sentences.business: Don't let processes work without proper permission.
sentences.formal: Written permission is required.
sentences.informal: Did he give you permission to do that?

words.usageNote: 'With/without permission'. 'Grant permission'. 'Ask for permission'.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to wait calmly

sentences.daily: Let patience guide your actions.
sentences.office: Don't let impatience ruin the negotiation.
sentences.interview: Let me demonstrate patience under pressure.
sentences.business: Don't let urgency kill patience.
sentences.formal: Patience is a virtue every professional must cultivate.
sentences.informal: Let him take his time — have some patience.

words.usageNote: 'Be patient' = adjective form. 'Patience' is uncountable.

words.simpleMeaning: to stop someone in the middle of speaking

sentences.daily: Don't let him interrupt others.
sentences.office: Please let me finish — don't interrupt.
sentences.interview: I don't let anyone interrupt the meeting flow.
sentences.business: Don't let minor issues interrupt operations.
sentences.formal: Interrupting the speaker is considered disrespectful.
sentences.informal: Don't interrupt — let her finish!

words.usageNote: 'Interrupt' someone. 'Without interruption' = continuously.

words.simpleMeaning: to slide accidentally or to go unnoticed

sentences.daily: Don't let the glass slip from your hand.
sentences.office: Don't let important emails slip through.
sentences.interview: Don't let a chance slip away.
sentences.business: Don't let profit margins slip.
sentences.formal: He let the deadline slip without notice.
sentences.informal: Don't let it slip — remind him daily.

words.usageNote: 'Let it slip' = accidentally reveal. 'Slip away' = go unnoticed.

words.simpleMeaning: a heavy load or responsibility

sentences.daily: Don't let worries burden you.
sentences.office: Don't let one person carry all the burden.
sentences.interview: I don't let stress burden my performance.
sentences.business: Don't let debt burden your business.
sentences.formal: The tax burden must be fairly distributed.
sentences.informal: Don't let him burden you with his problems.

words.usageNote: 'Be burdened with/by' something. 'Burden of proof' = legal term.

words.simpleMeaning: to try hard in difficult circumstances

sentences.daily: Don't let him struggle alone — help him.
sentences.office: Let the team identify where they struggle.
sentences.interview: I struggled initially but adapted quickly.
sentences.business: Don't let cash flow struggles sink the business.
sentences.formal: The organization struggled during the economic downturn.
sentences.informal: Let her struggle a little — it builds strength.

words.usageNote: 'Struggle with' a problem. 'Struggle to do' something difficult.

words.simpleMeaning: to go forward or continue

sentences.daily: Let me proceed with my plan.
sentences.office: Let the work proceed without interruptions.
sentences.interview: Please let me proceed — I'll be brief.
sentences.business: Let the deal proceed to the next stage.
sentences.formal: The project will proceed as planned.
sentences.informal: Let him proceed — we'll interrupt if needed.

words.usageNote: 'Proceed with/to': proceed with the plan. Formal alternative to 'continue'.

words.simpleMeaning: to officially choose someone for a job

sentences.daily: Let the family appoint a spokesperson.
sentences.office: Let the board appoint a new director.
sentences.interview: I was appointed project lead in my previous role.
sentences.business: Let us appoint an expert consultant.
sentences.formal: The committee appointed a three-member panel.
sentences.informal: Let him appoint whoever he trusts.

words.usageNote: 'Appoint as': She was appointed as head. 'Appointment' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something clearer

sentences.daily: Let me clarify what I mean.
sentences.office: Let her clarify the instructions before we start.
sentences.interview: Let me clarify my previous point.
sentences.business: Let us clarify the scope of work.
sentences.formal: The speaker clarified the ambiguous points.
sentences.informal: Let him clarify — he probably didn't mean it that way.

words.usageNote: 'Clarify' implies removing confusion. More formal than 'explain'.

words.simpleMeaning: to carry out or perform a task

sentences.daily: Let me execute this plan step by step.
sentences.office: Let the team execute the strategy.
sentences.interview: I execute tasks with full attention to detail.
sentences.business: Let us execute the launch campaign.
sentences.formal: The project was executed on time and within budget.
sentences.informal: Let him execute — he knows what he's doing.

words.usageNote: 'Execute a plan/task'. Different from 'execute' meaning death penalty — context matters.

words.simpleMeaning: to make someone believe something

sentences.daily: Let me convince you with facts.
sentences.office: Let the data convince the management.
sentences.interview: Let me convince you I am the right candidate.
sentences.business: Let the results convince potential investors.
sentences.formal: Evidence must convince the jury.
sentences.informal: Let him convince her — he's better at it.

words.usageNote: 'Convince someone to do' = persuade. 'Be convinced' = fully believe.

words.simpleMeaning: to help someone

sentences.daily: Let me assist you with the luggage.
sentences.office: Let the intern assist with research.
sentences.interview: I assist my team in solving complex problems.
sentences.business: Let our support team assist you.
sentences.formal: The officer assisted citizens during the crisis.
sentences.informal: Let her assist — she wants to help.

words.usageNote: 'Assist in/with': assist in completing. More formal than 'help'.

words.simpleMeaning: to examine something carefully

sentences.daily: Let me inspect the car before buying.
sentences.office: Let the quality team inspect the product.
sentences.interview: I inspect my own work before submitting.
sentences.business: Let an auditor inspect the accounts.
sentences.formal: The premises were inspected for compliance.
sentences.informal: Let him inspect it — he'll find any problem.

words.usageNote: 'Inspection' = noun. 'Inspect for' issues/defects.

words.simpleMeaning: to put forward a plan or idea formally

sentences.daily: Let me propose a solution.
sentences.office: Let her propose the agenda for the meeting.
sentences.interview: Let me propose how I'd approach this project.
sentences.business: Let us propose a partnership.
sentences.formal: The committee proposed a new resolution.
sentences.informal: Let him propose — it's his idea.

words.usageNote: 'Propose to do / propose doing'. 'Propose to someone' = ask them to marry.

words.simpleMeaning: to refuse to accept something

sentences.daily: Don't let rejection stop you from trying.
sentences.office: Don't let the manager reject good ideas without hearing them.
sentences.interview: I learn from every rejection.
sentences.business: Don't let the client reject the proposal without a counter-offer.
sentences.formal: The application was rejected due to incomplete documents.
sentences.informal: Don't let one rejection hurt you.

words.usageNote: 'Reject' (verb) vs 'rejection' (noun). 'Reject an idea/proposal'.

words.simpleMeaning: to formally present something

sentences.daily: Let me submit this form online.
sentences.office: Let him submit the report by end of day.
sentences.interview: I submitted my resume a week before the deadline.
sentences.business: Let us submit the bid today.
sentences.formal: All submissions must be made by noon.
sentences.informal: Don't let him submit without proofreading.

words.usageNote: 'Submit to' an authority. 'Submit for review'.

words.simpleMeaning: to make or become better

sentences.daily: Let me improve my English every day.
sentences.office: Let the team improve the workflow.
sentences.interview: Let me tell you how I improved my last project.
sentences.business: Don't let quality improvement be an afterthought.
sentences.formal: Processes were continuously improved over the year.
sentences.informal: Let him improve — give him time.

words.usageNote: 'Improve on' = do better than before. 'Improvement' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to succeed in dealing with a problem

sentences.daily: Let me overcome this fear step by step.
sentences.office: Let the team overcome this obstacle together.
sentences.interview: Let me tell you how I overcame a major challenge.
sentences.business: Don't let competition overcome your strengths.
sentences.formal: The organization overcame the crisis through planning.
sentences.informal: Let him overcome it himself — it'll build confidence.

words.usageNote: Irregular: overcome → overcame → overcome.

words.simpleMeaning: to recognize the value of something

sentences.daily: Let me appreciate all the small things.
sentences.office: Let the manager appreciate the team's effort.
sentences.interview: I appreciate feedback, both positive and critical.
sentences.business: Let satisfied customers appreciate your brand.
sentences.formal: The board appreciates the team's dedication.
sentences.informal: Let him know you appreciate his help.

words.usageNote: 'Appreciate' + noun or gerund: I appreciate your help / helping.

words.simpleMeaning: to achieve what you were trying to do

sentences.daily: Let hard work help you succeed.
sentences.office: Let the right strategy help the team succeed.
sentences.interview: I want to succeed in a role that challenges me.
sentences.business: Let customer focus help you succeed.
sentences.formal: The project succeeded due to excellent planning.
sentences.informal: Let him succeed — he has worked hard.

words.usageNote: 'Succeed in doing' = manage to do. 'Succeed someone' = take their place.

words.simpleMeaning: to work together toward a common goal

sentences.daily: Let everyone cooperate for the family function.
sentences.office: Let all departments cooperate on this project.
sentences.interview: I believe in cooperating across teams.
sentences.business: Let suppliers and buyers cooperate fairly.
sentences.formal: Parties agreed to cooperate on the investigation.
sentences.informal: Don't let pride stop you — cooperate with them.

words.usageNote: 'Cooperate with' someone. 'Cooperation' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to stop something that was planned

sentences.daily: Don't let him cancel the plan last minute.
sentences.office: Let management decide if we should cancel the event.
sentences.interview: I never cancel scheduled commitments.
sentences.business: Don't let clients cancel without understanding why.
sentences.formal: The event was cancelled due to unforeseen circumstances.
sentences.informal: Don't let her cancel again — this is the third time!

words.usageNote: British: 'cancelled'. American: 'canceled'. Both correct.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something known publicly

sentences.daily: Let me announce the good news.
sentences.office: Let the CEO announce the new policy.
sentences.interview: Let me announce my key achievement upfront.
sentences.business: Let us announce the product launch on Monday.
sentences.formal: The results were announced at the press conference.
sentences.informal: Don't let him announce it before I'm ready!

words.usageNote: 'Announce' to a group. 'Tell' is for one-on-one.

words.simpleMeaning: to say that something has faults

sentences.daily: Don't let him criticize others harshly.
sentences.office: Let constructive criticism improve the team.
sentences.interview: I welcome criticism as a tool for growth.
sentences.business: Don't let negativity criticize every idea.
sentences.formal: The audit report criticized several procedures.
sentences.informal: Let her criticize — it means she cares.

words.usageNote: 'Constructive criticism' = helpful. 'Destructive criticism' = harmful.

words.simpleMeaning: to cause someone to have a wrong impression

sentences.daily: Don't let advertisements mislead you.
sentences.office: Don't let unclear instructions mislead the team.
sentences.interview: I never let incomplete information mislead my decisions.
sentences.business: Don't let false data mislead your strategy.
sentences.formal: Misleading clients is against company policy.
sentences.informal: Don't let him mislead you — he's not honest.

words.usageNote: Irregular: mislead → misled → misled.

words.simpleMeaning: to help someone in a task

sentences.daily: Let him assist with the cooking.
sentences.office: Let the new joiner assist the senior analyst.
sentences.interview: I am happy to assist in any way needed.
sentences.business: Let our team assist your operations.
sentences.formal: She was asked to assist the department head.
sentences.informal: Let her assist — she wants to be useful.

words.usageNote: 'Assist with/in' = help with. More formal than 'help'.

words.simpleMeaning: to give guidance or recommendations

sentences.daily: Let me advise you on this decision.
sentences.office: Let the legal team advise on the contract.
sentences.interview: I advise my teammates when they need clarity.
sentences.business: Let experts advise on your financial planning.
sentences.formal: The board was advised to delay the decision.
sentences.informal: Let him advise you — he has experience.

words.usageNote: 'Advise someone to do': advise him to wait. 'Give advice' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to discuss to reach a mutual agreement

sentences.daily: Let me negotiate with the shopkeeper.
sentences.office: Let the sales manager negotiate the price.
sentences.interview: I negotiated a better salary in my last job.
sentences.business: Don't let the deal close without negotiating.
sentences.formal: Both parties negotiated the terms of the contract.
sentences.informal: Let him negotiate — he's sharp with numbers.

words.usageNote: 'Negotiate with' someone. 'Negotiate a deal/contract'.

words.simpleMeaning: to accept something unpleasant without complaint

sentences.daily: Don't let him tolerate bad behavior.
sentences.office: Don't let the company tolerate misconduct.
sentences.interview: I tolerate pressure and adapt quickly.
sentences.business: Don't let poor quality be tolerated.
sentences.formal: Zero tolerance for harassment is the company policy.
sentences.informal: Don't tolerate it — let him know it's wrong.

words.usageNote: 'Tolerate' implies reluctant acceptance. 'Tolerance' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep something in good condition or continue it

sentences.daily: Let me maintain my daily routine.
sentences.office: Let the team maintain quality standards.
sentences.interview: I maintain professionalism in all situations.
sentences.business: Let us maintain strong client relationships.
sentences.formal: Quality must be maintained at every stage.
sentences.informal: Don't let things fall apart — maintain the effort.

words.usageNote: 'Maintain' implies consistent effort. 'Maintenance' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to show or prove clearly

sentences.daily: Let me demonstrate how to do it.
sentences.office: Let the trainer demonstrate the new software.
sentences.interview: Let me demonstrate my skills through examples.
sentences.business: Let our product demonstrate its own value.
sentences.formal: The experiment demonstrated the theory.
sentences.informal: Let him demonstrate — he knows the process.

words.usageNote: 'Demonstrate how to' = show the method. 'Demonstration' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to suggest something as good or suitable

sentences.daily: Let me recommend a good book.
sentences.office: Let the senior recommend the best vendor.
sentences.interview: Let me recommend myself for this position.
sentences.business: Don't let customers leave without a recommendation.
sentences.formal: The doctor recommended rest for a week.
sentences.informal: Let him recommend — he knows the best places.

words.usageNote: 'Recommend + ing / that': recommend reading / that he reads.

words.simpleMeaning: to pause before doing something due to uncertainty

sentences.daily: Don't let fear make you hesitate.
sentences.office: Don't hesitate — let him know your opinion.
sentences.interview: Please don't hesitate to ask me anything.
sentences.business: Don't let clients hesitate — simplify the decision.
sentences.formal: Hesitation can lead to missed opportunities.
sentences.informal: Don't hesitate — just go for it!

words.usageNote: 'Hesitate to do': Don't hesitate to ask.

words.simpleMeaning: to make ready for something

sentences.daily: Let me prepare dinner for everyone.
sentences.office: Let the team prepare a presentation.
sentences.interview: Let me prepare you for the toughest question.
sentences.business: Let us prepare a detailed proposal.
sentences.formal: All documents must be prepared before the audit.
sentences.informal: Don't let him go unprepared — help him.

words.usageNote: 'Prepare for' = get ready for. 'Prepare + object': prepare a report.

words.simpleMeaning: to stop being angry at someone for something bad

sentences.daily: Let me forgive and move on.
sentences.office: Let the team forgive small mistakes.
sentences.interview: I forgive quickly and focus on solutions.
sentences.business: Don't let resentment ruin a business relationship.
sentences.formal: The court was asked to consider a pardon.
sentences.informal: Let him forgive her — they're family.

words.usageNote: Irregular: forgive → forgave → forgiven. 'Forgive someone for something'.

words.simpleMeaning: to work together with others

sentences.daily: Let all neighbors cooperate for the event.
sentences.office: Let departments cooperate for better results.
sentences.interview: I always cooperate with cross-functional teams.
sentences.business: Let us cooperate to serve clients better.
sentences.formal: International parties cooperated on the agreement.
sentences.informal: Just cooperate — don't make it difficult.

words.usageNote: 'Cooperate with' someone on something.

words.simpleMeaning: to provide help in doing something

sentences.daily: Let me assist you with directions.
sentences.office: Let the team assist the client onsite.
sentences.interview: I assisted the senior manager on three projects.
sentences.business: Let our experts assist your team.
sentences.formal: The officer was asked to assist with crowd management.
sentences.informal: Don't hesitate — let me assist you.

words.usageNote: Formal synonym of 'help'. 'Assistant' = noun (a person who assists).

words.simpleMeaning: to investigate or examine something new

sentences.daily: Let me explore this new neighborhood.
sentences.office: Let the team explore creative solutions.
sentences.interview: Let me explore what this role can offer.
sentences.business: Let us explore new markets.
sentences.formal: The committee explored various options.
sentences.informal: Let him explore — he'll figure it out.

words.usageNote: 'Explore options/opportunities'. Also used literally for physical exploration.

words.simpleMeaning: to break in on a conversation or activity

sentences.daily: Don't let him interrupt the storytelling.
sentences.office: Please don't interrupt — let her finish.
sentences.interview: I don't let interruptions affect my composure.
sentences.business: Don't let technical issues interrupt the demo.
sentences.formal: The session was interrupted by a fire alarm.
sentences.informal: Stop interrupting — let him speak!

words.usageNote: Rude without permission. 'Excuse me for interrupting' is polite.

words.simpleMeaning: to travel or go with someone

sentences.daily: Let me accompany my grandmother to the doctor.
sentences.office: Let the assistant accompany the client.
sentences.interview: I accompanied my manager on 5 field visits.
sentences.business: Let our sales rep accompany you to the demo.
sentences.formal: The minister was accompanied by security.
sentences.informal: Let him accompany you — he knows the area.

words.usageNote: Formal than 'go with'. Often used in professional and official contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: to examine or assess something carefully

sentences.daily: Let me review my notes before the exam.
sentences.office: Let the manager review the report.
sentences.interview: Let me review my achievements for you.
sentences.business: Let us review the contract clause by clause.
sentences.formal: The committee reviewed all submissions.
sentences.informal: Don't let him sign without reviewing it.

words.usageNote: 'Review' as noun = a formal assessment or critique.

words.simpleMeaning: to continue doing something despite difficulty

sentences.daily: Let determination persist when things get hard.
sentences.office: Don't let problems persist without a solution.
sentences.interview: I persist until I find the right answer.
sentences.business: Don't let inefficiencies persist unchecked.
sentences.formal: The issue persisted for over three months.
sentences.informal: Don't let him persist — he needs to change.

words.usageNote: 'Persist in doing': persist in trying. 'Persistent' = adjective.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something easy to understand

sentences.daily: Let me clarify this point once more.
sentences.office: Let the supervisor clarify the task.
sentences.interview: Let me clarify my answer.
sentences.business: Don't let ambiguity persist — clarify now.
sentences.formal: The spokesperson clarified the official stance.
sentences.informal: Let her clarify — she was misquoted.

words.usageNote: 'Clarify' removes doubt. More precise than 'explain'.

words.simpleMeaning: to cause a break in continuity

sentences.daily: Don't let power cuts interrupt your study.
sentences.office: Don't let system errors interrupt work.
sentences.interview: I make sure nothing interrupts my preparation.
sentences.business: Don't let supply chain issues interrupt production.
sentences.formal: Services were not interrupted during the upgrade.
sentences.informal: Don't let the noise interrupt him.

words.usageNote: In business/tech: 'interruption in service'. In social: 'don't interrupt'.

words.simpleMeaning: to formally give or allow something

sentences.daily: Don't take your blessings for granted.
sentences.office: Let the management grant extra leave.
sentences.interview: I was granted an opportunity to lead a team.
sentences.business: Let the bank grant the loan.
sentences.formal: The court granted bail to the accused.
sentences.informal: Don't let him take you for granted.

words.usageNote: 'Take for granted' = not appreciate. 'Be granted' = receive officially.

words.simpleMeaning: to arrange for something to happen later than planned

sentences.daily: Don't let him postpone his health checkup.
sentences.office: Let us postpone the review to next Monday.
sentences.interview: I never postpone difficult conversations.
sentences.business: Don't let launches be postponed unnecessarily.
sentences.formal: The meeting has been postponed indefinitely.
sentences.informal: Don't let him postpone again!

words.usageNote: 'Postpone to/until'. Synonyms: put off (informal), defer (formal).

words.simpleMeaning: to accept or confess something

sentences.daily: Let him admit his mistake.
sentences.office: Let the employee admit the error and fix it.
sentences.interview: I readily admit what I don't know.
sentences.business: Don't let pride stop you from admitting a mistake.
sentences.formal: The defendant admitted to the charges.
sentences.informal: Let her admit it — everyone already knows.

words.usageNote: 'Admit to' + noun/gerund. 'Admission' = noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to give up or accept defeat

sentences.daily: Don't let difficulties make you surrender your dreams.
sentences.office: Don't let setbacks make you surrender.
sentences.interview: I never surrender to failure — I learn.
sentences.business: Don't let the market make you surrender.
sentences.formal: The party surrendered its claim to the property.
sentences.informal: Don't let him surrender so easily!

words.usageNote: 'Surrender to' something. Can be literal (war) or metaphorical.

words.simpleMeaning: to give special importance to something

sentences.daily: Let me emphasize how important this is.
sentences.office: Let the manager emphasize quality over speed.
sentences.interview: Let me emphasize my communication skills.
sentences.business: Don't let your USP go unemphasized.
sentences.formal: The report emphasized the need for urgent action.
sentences.informal: Let him emphasize the key points.

words.usageNote: British: 'emphasise'. 'Emphasis' = noun. 'Place emphasis on'.

words.simpleMeaning: to create or set something up

sentences.daily: Let me establish a daily routine.
sentences.office: Let us establish clear roles and responsibilities.
sentences.interview: I established a new process that saved 20% time.
sentences.business: Let your brand establish trust before selling.
sentences.formal: The committee was established in 2018.
sentences.informal: Let him establish himself first.

words.usageNote: 'Establish' something (an organization, a fact, a routine). More formal than 'start'.

words.simpleMeaning: to ask for advice from an expert

sentences.daily: Let me consult a doctor first.
sentences.office: Let the team consult the legal department.
sentences.interview: I consult stakeholders before major decisions.
sentences.business: Let clients consult our experts for free.
sentences.formal: All employees should consult the HR policy.
sentences.informal: Let him consult his dad — he's wise.

words.usageNote: 'Consult with' someone. 'Consultant' = a person who gives expert advice.

words.simpleMeaning: to find a solution to a problem

sentences.daily: Let me resolve this issue right now.
sentences.office: Let the team resolve conflicts quickly.
sentences.interview: Let me tell you how I resolved a crisis.
sentences.business: Let us resolve this before the client notices.
sentences.formal: The dispute was resolved through mediation.
sentences.informal: Just let them resolve it between themselves.

words.usageNote: 'Resolve to do' = make a firm decision. 'Resolve a dispute/issue'.

words.simpleMeaning: to fill someone with the urge to do something creative or great

sentences.daily: Let his story inspire you to work harder.
sentences.office: Let the manager inspire the team.
sentences.interview: Let me share a person who inspired me.
sentences.business: Let your mission inspire your team.
sentences.formal: The founder's vision inspired a generation of entrepreneurs.
sentences.informal: Don't let anyone stop you — let passion inspire you.

words.usageNote: 'Inspire someone to do': inspired me to become a teacher.
